heading: Gulf Islands National Seashore
label: 459h Gulf Islands National Seashore
notes: References in TextSections 459h to 459h–10 of this title, referred to in subsec. (b)(3), was in the original “this title”, and was translated as reading “this Act”, meaning , which enacted sections 459h to 459h–10 of this title, to reflect the probable intent of Congress, because does not contain titles.Pub. L. 91–660Pub. L. 91–660

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: In order to preserve for public use and enjoyment certain areas possessing outstanding natural, historic, and recreational values, the Secretary of the Interior (hereinafter referred to as the “Secretary”) may establish and administer the Gulf Islands National Seashore (hereinafter referred to as the “seashore”).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The seashore shall comprise the areas described in paragraphs (2) and (3).

children.heading: Areas included in boundary plan numbered NS–GI–7100J
children.label: 2 Areas included in boundary plan numbered NS–GI–7100J
A
children.text: Ship, Petit Bois, and Horn Islands in Mississippi;

children.label: B
children.text: the eastern portion of Perdido Key in Florida;

children.label: D
children.text: the Naval Live Oaks Reservation in Florida;

children.label: E
children.text: Fort Pickens and the Fort Pickens State Park in Florida; and

children.label: F
children.text: a tract of land in the Pensacola Naval Air Station in Florida that includes the Coast Guard Station and Lighthouse, Fort San Carlos, Fort Barrancas, and Fort Redoubt and sufficient surrounding land for proper administration and protection of the historic resources.

children.heading: Cat Island
children.label: 3 Cat Island
children.text: Upon its acquisition by the Secretary, the area described in this paragraph is the parcel consisting of approximately 2,000 acres of land on Cat Island, Mississippi, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Boundary Map, Gulf Islands National Seashore, Cat Island, Mississippi”, numbered 635/80085, and dated (referred to in sections 459h to 459h–10 of this title as the “Cat Island Map”).November 9, 19991See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Availability of Map
children.label: 4 Availability of Map
children.text: The Cat Island Map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service.
